description: Hard-coded SEVA orbit camera preset that was previously baked into dust3r_reconstructor.py. This preset forces DUSt3R to use a perfect orbit trajectory instead of solving poses from image matches. It was tuned for SEVA-generated small-baseline orbit views (e.g. backend/duster_images).
note: To re-apply, paste the 'code_block' into Dust3rReconstructor.reconstruct() right after global_aligner(...) is created and before compute_global_alignment().
code_block:
if mode == GlobalAlignerMode.PointCloudOptimizer:
            logger.info("Computing global alignment (Optimization steps)...")
            # Hand DUSt3R the SEVA orbit poses + intrinsics so it only refines points,
            # not camera pose (otherwise small-baseline NVS frames are ill-conditioned).
            from seva.geometry import get_preset_pose_fov, get_default_intrinsics
            c2ws, fovs = get_preset_pose_fov(
                option="orbit", num_frames=len(imgs) + 1,
                start_w2c=torch.eye(4), look_at=torch.Tensor([0, 0, 10]),
            )
            H, W = imgs[0]['img'].shape[-2], imgs[0]['img'].shape[-1]
            Ks = get_default_intrinsics(fovs, aspect_ratio=W / H)
            Ks[:, :2] *= torch.tensor([W, H]).reshape(1, -1, 1).repeat(Ks.shape[0], 1, 1)
            scene.preset_pose(list(np.asarray(c2ws)[1:1 + len(imgs)]))
            Ks_use = Ks[1:1 + len(imgs)]
            scene.preset_focal([K.diagonal()[:2].mean().item() for K in Ks_use])
            # NOTE: skipping preset_principal_point — DUSt3R's PointCloudOptimizer
            # defaults optimize_pp=False, which already pins pp to (W/2, H/2),
            # the exact value SEVA's get_default_intrinsics produces.
            loss = scene.compute_global_alignment(init='known_poses', niter=300, schedule='linear', lr=0.01)
when_to_use: When input images are SEVA-generated orbit views with small baselines where DUSt3R's own pose solver is ill-conditioned. Not recommended for general image sets with arbitrary camera motion.
